Be specific about what data is needed.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":[],"type":"object"},"name":"missing_data"},{"description":"Signal that the task could not be completed due to an infrastructure or tool failure (e.g., MCP server crash, missing authentication, inaccessible repository). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"message":{"description":"Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["message"],"type":"object"},"name":"noop"},{"description":"Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"alternatives":{"description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"context":{"description":"Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"data_type":{"description":"Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":[],"type":"object"},"name":"missing_data"},{"description":"Signal that the task could not be completed due to an infrastructure or tool failure (e.g., MCP server crash, missing authentication, inaccessible repository). Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"details":{"description":"Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). Be specific about which tools or resources were unavailable.","type":"string"}},"required":["reason"],"type":"object"},"name":"report_incomplete"},{"description":"Post a routing comment @mentioning team owners on the triggering issue; bypasses safe-outputs mention neutralization","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"message":{"description":"The comment body text without any @mentions or @ symbols","type":"string"},"owners":{"description":"Comma-separated GitHub usernames to notify, without the @ prefix (e.g. 'user1, user2, Azure/team-name')","type":"string"}},"required":["message","owners"],"type":"object"},"name":"mention_owners"}]}}}
2026-04-08T16:14:35.3162784Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-08T16:11:50.408547892Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"tools":[{"annotations":{"readOnlyHint":true,"title":"Get a specific label from a repository."},"description":"Get a specific label from a repository.","icons":[{"mimeType":"image/png","src":"data:image/png;base64,","theme":"light"},{"mimeType":"image/png","src":"data:image/png;base64,","theme":"dark"}],"inputSchema":{"properties":{"name":{"description":"Label name.","type":"string"},"owner":{"description":"Repository owner (username or organization name)","type":"string"},"repo":{"description":"Repository name","type":"string"}},"required":["owner","repo","name"],"type":"object"},"name":"get_label"},{"annotations":{"readOnlyHint":true,"title":"Get issue details"},"description":"Get information about a specific issue in a GitHub repository.","icons":[{"mimeType":"image/png","src":"data:image/png;base64,","theme":"light"},{"mimeType":"image/png","src":"data:image/png;base64,","theme":"dark"}],"inputSchema":{"properties":{"issue_number":{"description":"The number of the issue","type":"number"},"method":{"description":"The read operation to perform on a single issue.\nOptions are:\n1. get - Get details of a specific issue.\n2. get_comments - Get issue comments.\n3. get_sub_issues - Get sub-issues of the issue.\n4. get_labels - Get labels assigned to the issue.\n","enum":["get","get_comments","get_sub_issues","get_labels"],"type":"string"},"owner":{"description":"The owner of the repository","type":"string"},"page":{"description":"Page number for pagination (min 1)","minimum":1,"type":"number"},"perPage":{"description":"Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)","maximum":100,"minimum":1,"type":"number"},"repo":{"description":"The name of the repository","type":"string"}},"required":["method","owner","repo","issue_number"],"type":"object"},"name":"issue_read"},{"annotations":{"readOnlyHint":true,"title":"List available issue types"},"description":"List supported issue types for repository owner (organization).","icons":[{"mimeType":"image/png","src":"data:image/png;base64,","theme":"light"},{"mimeType":"image/png","src":"data:image/png;base64,","theme":"dark"}],"inputSchema":{"properties":{"owner":{"description":"The organization owner of the repository","type":"string"}},"required":["owner"],"type":"object"},"name":"list_issue_types"},{"annotations":{"readOnlyHint":true,"title":"List issues"},"description":"List issues in a GitHub repository. For pagination, use the 'endCursor' from the previous response's 'pageInfo' in the 'after' parameter.","icons":[{"mimeType":"image/png","src":"data:image/png;base64,","theme":"light"},{"mimeType":"image/png","src":"data:image/png;base64,","theme":"dark"}],"inputSchema":{"properties":{"after":{"description":"Cursor for pagination. Use the endCursor from the previous page's PageInfo for GraphQL APIs.","type":"string"},"direction":{"description":"Order direction. If provided, the 'orderBy' also needs to be provided.","enum":["ASC","DESC"],"type":"string"},"labels":{"description":"Filter by labels","items":{"type":"string"},"type":"array"},"orderBy":{"description":"Order issues by field. If provided, the 'direction' also needs to be provided.","enum":["CREATED_AT","UPDATED_AT","COMMENTS"],"type":"string"},"owner":{"description":"Repository owner","type":"string"},"perPage":{"description":"Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)","maximum":100,"minimum":1,"type":"number"},"repo":{"description":"Repository name","type":"string"},"since":{"description":"Filter by date (ISO 8601 timestamp)","type":"string"},"state":{"description":"Filter by state, by default both open and closed issues are returned when not provided","enum":["OPEN","CLOSED"],"type":"string"}},"required":["owner","repo"],"type":"object"},"name":"list_issues"},{"annotations":{"readOnlyHint":true,"title":"Search issues"},"description":"Search for issues in GitHub repositories using issues search syntax already scoped to is:issue","icons":[{"mimeType":"image/png","src":"data:image/png;base64,","theme":"light"},{"mimeType":"image/png","src":"data:image/png;base64,","theme":"dark"}],"inputSchema":{"properties":{"order":{"description":"Sort order","enum":["asc","desc"],"type":"string"},"owner":{"description":"Optional repository owner. 2026-04-08T16:14:35.3252526Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-08T16:12:51.38073706Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":57940,\"title\":\"[GeneratorAgent] Docs: Document TestResult type and missing MCP tools in README\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `Azure.GeneratorAgent`\\n**Service directory:** `sdk/tools/Azure.GeneratorAgent/`\\n**Triggered by:** commit [REDACTED] (#57912) by `@radhgupta`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nPR #57912 force-tracked `TestResult.cs` which was previously ignored by a `.gitignore` pattern. This file defines a new public `TestResult` class — the structured return type of the `run_tests` MCP tool — with properties for `Success`, `ExitCode`, `Passed`, `Failed`, `Skipped`, `Total`, `Failures`, `Error`, and `RawOutput`.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- `TestResult` is a public API type with 9 properties but is not mentioned anywhere in `README.md` or `CHANGELOG.md`\\n- Three MCP tool implementations exist on disk but are entirely absent from the README\\u0026#39;s MCP Tools table: `AddCodeGenSuppressTool` (`add_codegen_suppress`), `DiscoverProjectTool` (`discover_project`), and `GeneratedSnapshotTool` (`generated_snapshot`)\\n- The README text states \\u0026#34;18 individual tools\\u0026#34; but the table lists only 16 — the count is now inconsistent with both the table and the actual implementation\\n- The `CHANGELOG.md` for `1.0.0-beta.1 (Unreleased)` does not mention the `TestResult` structured result type as a feature addition\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n**File 1: `sdk/tools/Azure.GeneratorAgent/README.md`** — Edit\\n\\nIn the **MCP Tools** section, replace the table (currently 16 rows) with an updated table that includes the 3 missing tools and fixes the count:\\n\\n1. Update the introductory sentence from \\u0026#34;18 individual tools\\u0026#34; to \\u0026#34;19 individual tools\\u0026#34; (or the actual count after auditing all `*Tool.cs` files).\\n2. Add the following rows to the MCP Tools table (insert in alphabetical order by tool name):\\n\\n```markdown\\n| `add_codegen_suppress` | Add a `[CodeGenSuppress]` attribute to a type member in a customization file |\\n| `discover_project` | Discover the SDK project path and metadata for a given service directory |\\n| `generated_snapshot` | Take a snapshot of the Generated/ folder contents for before/after comparison |\\n```\\n\\n3. After the MCP Tools table, add a new subsection documenting the structured result types:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Result Types\\n\\nThe following types are returned by MCP tools and can be inspected by the calling skill:\\n\\n#### `TestResult`\\n\\nReturned by `run_tests`. Contains a structured summary of a `dotnet test` run:\\n\\n| Property | Type | Description |\\n|----------|------|-------------|\\n| `Success` | `bool` | `true` if all tests passed (exit code 0) |\\n| `ExitCode` | `int` | Raw process exit code from `dotnet test` |\\n| `Passed` | `int` | Number of tests that passed |\\n| `Failed` | `int` | Number of tests that failed |\\n| `Skipped` | `int` | Number of tests that were skipped |\\n| `Total` | `int` | Total number of tests executed |\\n| `Failures` | `List` | Human-readable descriptions of each failed test |\\n| `Error` | `string?` | Top-level error message if the run itself failed (e.g., build error before tests ran) |\\n| `RawOutput` | `string` | Full raw stdout/stderr from the `dotnet test` process |\\n```\\n\\n**File 2: `sdk/tools/Azure.GeneratorAgent/CHANGELOG.md`** — Edit\\n\\nUnder `## 1.0.0-beta.1 (Unreleased)` → `### Features Added`, append:\\n\\n```markdown\\n- Added `TestResult` structured type returned by the `run_tests` MCP tool, exposing `Success`, `ExitCode`, `Passed`, `Failed`, `Skipped`, `Total`, `Failures`, `Error`, and `RawOutput` properties for programmatic inspection of test run outcomes\\n- Added `add_codegen_suppress` tool for inserting `[CodeGenSuppress]` attributes into customization files\\n- Added `discover_project` tool for locating SDK project metadata from a service directory path\\n- Added `generated_snapshot` tool for capturing before/after snapshots of the `Generated/` folder\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nNo new snippet placeholders are required for this change. The `TestResult` type documentation can be added as a plain Markdown table in the README (not a code snippet extracted from a test file), since it is reference documentation rather than executable usage.\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe README at `sdk/tools/Azure.GeneratorAgent/README.md` already contains all six required sections in order:\\n1. ✅ Getting started (install, prerequisites, build)\\n2. ✅ Key concepts (architecture, MCP tools table, fix registry)\\n3. ✅ Examples (migration skill, Copilot Chat usage)\\n4. ✅ Troubleshooting (common issues)\\n5. ✅ Contributing (adding new fix rules)\\n6. ✅ Next steps (external links)\\n\\nThe only changes needed are within the existing **Key concepts → MCP Tools** subsection (add missing tool rows and fix count) and adding a new **Result Types** subsection under **Key concepts**.\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order:\\n\\n1. `dotnet build sdk/tools/Azure.GeneratorAgent/src/`\\n2. `dotnet test sdk/tools/Azure.GeneratorAgent/tests/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\\n3. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 tools` — regenerates the public API listing; `TestResult` being public means the API surface changed\\n4. `dotnet format sdk/tools/Azure.GeneratorAgent/src/`\\n\\nNo snippet update is needed (`Update-Snippets.ps1` is not required here).\\n\\n\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u0026gt;
